name: shaper-instrument-control
template_usage: Concrete Instrument Control v1 manifest for the Shaper as implemented today in digital-beacon (osc_receiver.py). Documents existing wire behaviour; does not invent new addresses.

listeners.socket_options: SO_REUSEPORT + SO_REUSEADDR so Shaper co-listens with the NH visualizer
listeners.description: NaturalHarmony harmonic_beacon broadcasts consumed as slave input: /beacon/voice/on|off|freq, /beacon/f1, /beacon/panic, /beacon/level.

hashes.algorithm: SHA-256 truncated to 128 bits, lowercase hex (32 characters)
hashes.contract_id: Canonical UTF-8 JSON with sorted keys, no whitespace and no NaN/Infinity, excluding top-level contract_id, golden_hash and expected_contract_id fields. The expected value lives in a golden sidecar.

instrument.description: Additive harmonic synthesizer (Shaper): up to 32 sine voices keyed by harmonic_n 1..32, waveshaper drive, per-voice LFO, beacon sidechain, master gain. Currently embedded in digital-beacon; this manifest formalizes the OSC surface already exposed by digital_beacon/osc_receiver.py.
instrument.native_namespace_rule: Native write namespace is /digital on UDP :9002. NH lifecycle and f1 arrive under /beacon on UDP :9001 as slave input — not flattened into /digital.

stream_identity.stream_id: Not implemented today. Planned: random 64-bit lowercase hexadecimal value generated once per instrument process start.
stream_identity.scope: When implemented, state_seq and monotonic client state are scoped to stream_id.
stream_identity.restart_rule: A new stream_id invalidates cached state and requires a fresh state synchronization.

counters.state_seq: Not implemented today. Planned: unsigned monotonic OSC int64 (h), incremented for every state change or state snapshot emitted by the instrument.

handshake.rebroadcast: Not implemented. Planned: at process start, approximately every hello_rebroadcast_s, and whenever contract or producer metadata changes. digital-beacon osc_receiver.py currently has no /hello or /hello/request handlers.

capabilities.name: harmonic_gain

arguments.name: gain
arguments.polarity: 0 = silent; 1 = maximum per-voice gain. Clamped in VoiceParameterStore.set_gain.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write; audio envelope (attack_s / release_s) applies only to voice on/off amplitude, not to this gain set.

capabilities.name: harmonic_envelope

arguments.name: gain
arguments.polarity: Positive values activate and control an owned voice at f1*N; 0 releases only that native envelope voice.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write; the audio envelope applies to the voice lifecycle. The controlled voice frequency tracks f1*N when f1 or vsrate changes.

arguments.name: pan
arguments.polarity: -1 = left; 0 = center; 1 = right. Clamped in VoiceParameterStore.set_pan.

capabilities.name: harmonic_phase

arguments.name: phase_degrees
arguments.polarity: OSC argument is degrees. Store converts with math.radians(phase_deg % 360). Increasing values advance phase; 360 wraps to 0.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write; no circular interpolation on the OSC path today.

arguments.name: gain
arguments.polarity: 0 = silent master; 1 = full Shaper master gain. Clamped in set_master_gain. Default DEFAULT_SHAPER_MASTER = 0.8.

capabilities.name: partial_ceiling

arguments.name: level
arguments.polarity: 0 = only fundamental (n_max=1); 1 = full grid (n_max=32). Mapped as n_max = 1 + round(level * 31). Clamped in VoiceParameterStore.set_partial_ceiling_from_level.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write. Audio callback forces target_env=0 for harmonic_n > partial_ceiling so high partials enter natural release (attack_s/release_s ramp); rising ceiling re-enables them. Scene parameter — panic does not reset it.

capabilities.name: clock_bpm

arguments.name: bpm
arguments.polarity: Musical clock tempo in BPM. Clamped in VoiceParameterStore.set_clock_bpm. Default 90. Shaper-owned; Weaver may converge this from body tempo. Beat phase advances at bpm/60 cycles per second.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write. Beat phase accumulator (_beat_phase 0..1) uses the new rate on subsequent advance_beat/advance_lfo calls. Scene parameter — panic does not reset it.

capabilities.name: settle_beats

arguments.name: beats
arguments.polarity: Ease time constant in local musical beats (not ms). Clamped in VoiceParameterStore.set_settle_beats. Default 1.0. Used by eased_target(current, target, delta_beats, settle_beats).

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write. Convergence itself is exponential ease over local beats (1 - exp(-delta_beats/settle_beats)), network-robust because time is musical not wall-clock. Scene parameter — panic does not reset it.

capabilities.name: generator_enable

arguments.name: enable
arguments.polarity: 0 = generators (arpegio/perc) off; 1 = on. Default 1. Stored as bool in VoiceParameterStore.set_generator_enable.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write. Gate for Shaper-owned generators; does not silence harmonic envelope voices. Scene parameter — panic does not reset it.

capabilities.name: arp_enable

arguments.name: enable
arguments.polarity: 0 = this arpeggiator hand off; 1 = on. MVP implements H=0. Voice band -20000-H*1000-n.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate store write. Hand also gated by /digital/generator/enable.

capabilities.name: arp_rate

arguments.name: steps_per_beat
arguments.polarity: Steps per musical beat. 0 = sustain single note at window center. Eased over settle_beats.

capabilities.smoothing: Target write; audio callback eases settled_rate with eased_target over settle_beats.

capabilities.name: arp_direction

arguments.name: dir
arguments.polarity: -1 = downward cursor; +1 = upward. Zero-crossing of settled direction re-triggers a step.

capabilities.smoothing: Target write; eased over settle_beats. Sign of settled value drives cursor step.

capabilities.name: arp_density

arguments.name: fill
arguments.polarity: 0 = no subdivision (factor 1); 1 = max subdivision (factor 8). Quantized to {1,2,3,4,6,8}.

capabilities.smoothing: Target write; eased over settle_beats. Effective step rate = rate * subdiv(density).

capabilities.name: arp_register_lo

arguments.name: n
arguments.polarity: Lower edge of this hand's partial window (1..32). Effective window is [lo,hi] ∩ [1,ceiling].

capabilities.smoothing: Target write; eased over settle_beats then rounded to integer partial.

capabilities.name: arp_register_hi

arguments.name: n
arguments.polarity: Upper edge of this hand's partial window (1..32). Clamped by partial_ceiling in the callback.

capabilities.smoothing: Target write; eased over settle_beats then rounded to integer partial.

arguments.name: frac
arguments.polarity: Fraction of step period the note stays on (staccato↔legato). voice_off scheduled after gate*step_period.

arguments.name: g
arguments.polarity: Arpeggiator hand gain 0..1 applied to each triggered voice.

capabilities.name: panic

arguments.name: trigger
arguments.polarity: Message presence triggers panic; argument values are ignored by the handler.

capabilities.smoothing: Immediate: deactivates all voices, resets gain/pan/phase defaults, clears active history; may invoke MIDI panic callback. Does not reset scene parameters such as partial_ceiling.

slave_broadcasts.description: Addresses the Shaper listens to on :9001 as a NaturalHarmony broadcast slave. Not part of the native /digital write surface for Instrument Control clients, but part of the real OSC receive table in osc_receiver.py.

arguments.name: freq
arguments.polarity: Hz. Sets voice frequency and activates the harmonic.

arguments.name: source_note
arguments.polarity: Used as harmonic_n when harmonic_n arg is omitted.

arguments.name: harmonic_n
arguments.polarity: If present, overrides source_note as the harmonic index (1..32).

addresses.effect: store.voice_on(harmonic_n, voice_id, freq, gain); records a strum timestamp for LFO period estimation.
store.voice_off(voice_id) — deactivates the voice currently bound to that voice_id.

arguments.name: freq
arguments.polarity: Hz.

arguments.name: f1
arguments.polarity: Base fundamental Hz. Clamped to F1_MIN..F1_MAX (20..200); then multiplied by vsrate.

arguments.name: trigger
arguments.polarity: Arguments ignored; message presence triggers store.panic().

arguments.name: level
arguments.polarity: Beacon envelope level for sidechain (0..1). Clamped in set_beacon_level.

state_sync.status: partial — current mechanism is WebSocket; OSC dump is planned

current_mechanism.on_connect: Server accepts the socket and immediately sends store.to_dict() as JSON.
current_mechanism.on_change: VoiceParameterStore._on_change broadcasts store.to_dict() to all connected WebSocket clients (wired in api.py create_app).

planned.note: Planned OSC state dump at /shaper/state (extraction target namespace). Not implemented in digital-beacon today. Instrument Control request_address under the current native namespace is /digital/state for schema compliance; both are planned, not live.

state_sync.response: TODAY: WebSocket /ws delivers JSON snapshots (see current_mechanism). PLANNED OSC dump: one atomic bundle beginning with /digital/state/begin(stream_id, state_seq, contract_id) or the planned /shaper/state equivalent, followed by readable capability values at native addresses, ending with state/end(stream_id, state_seq).
state_sync.connect_rule: TODAY: open WebSocket /ws and apply the initial JSON snapshot before presenting state. After handshake is implemented, a client MUST complete a matching /hello and then either WS sync or the planned OSC dump before presenting or modifying instrument state.

receiver_rules.client_gating: TODAY: none — any UDP peer may write /digital/* on :9002 and /beacon/* on :9001; there is no contract_id gate. PLANNED: a client MUST NOT send capability writes until /hello contract_id exactly matches the locally accepted contract_id and state synchronization completes.
receiver_rules.stale_contract: TODAY: not applicable (no handshake). PLANNED: on a missing or mismatched contract_id, stop writes, discard state messages, mark the instrument incompatible and request /hello again.
receiver_rules.instrument_gating: TODAY: none — osc_receiver accepts all mapped addresses with a silent default handler for unknown paths. PLANNED: when a transport adapter can identify negotiated clients, ignore writes from clients that did not complete a matching handshake.
receiver_rules.monotonic_discard: TODAY: not applicable (no state_seq on the wire). PLANNED: within one stream_id, discard state updates with state_seq less than or equal to the last applied state_seq, except members of the same atomic state dump.
receiver_rules.stream_change: TODAY: process restart clears in-memory VoiceParameterStore; clients should re-open /ws. PLANNED: a new stream_id clears cached state and requires a fresh dump or broadcast synchronization.
receiver_rules.path_parameter_n: For /digital/harmonic/{N}/*, N is parsed as path segment index 3 (0-based split). Non-integer N is ignored. Store keys are harmonic_n in 1..32 (N_BANDS / MAX_VOICES).
receiver_rules.clamping: Numeric writes are clamped in VoiceParameterStore (gain 0..1, pan -1..1, phase_deg mod 360, master 0..1, f1 20..200, beacon_level 0..1).

voice_model_alias.status: Enabled — the Shaper fits the BCP common-voice model: per-voice gain/pan/phase over harmonic_n 1..32, plus f1 and on/off lifecycle (on/off and f1 arrive via NH /beacon/* slave path, not under /digital).

lifecycle_and_tuning.note: Lifecycle and f1 are NH broadcast inputs on :9001; they are documented in slave_broadcasts, not in the native /digital capability write set.

rest_surface_note: REST /api/shaper/* exposes additional parameters (shape, per-voice and global LFO, attack/release, sidechain) that have NO OSC addresses in osc_receiver.py today. They are out of the OSC capability table but part of the live control surface via HTTP + WebSocket state.
discrepancies_vs_report: Report 04-digital-beacon.md §1/§5 correctly names dual ports :9001 (/beacon/*) and :9002 (/digital/*) — matches code.
harmonic-shaper README currently says future namespace /shaper/*; live code uses /digital/* — CODE wins; /shaper/state dump is only planned.
REST exposes shape/LFO/envelope/sidechain; OSC direct control is gain/pan/phase/master/ceiling/panic — report mentions waveshaper/LFO/sidechain as engine features, not as OSC addresses.
Template/common-voice examples often use N bounds 0..31; Shaper uses harmonic_n 1..32 — CODE wins.
